title: What I think about Meghan Markle - Request

enrichment.summary: Kyle shares his thoughts on Meghan Markle marrying Prince Harry, covering her mixed-race background, the British Royal Family's historical significance, and why he thinks her presence is positive for both the monarchy and civil rights. He also offers unsolicited advice on how she should embrace her Black identity publicly and how Harry and Meghan should manage her acting career transition.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Supports it; sees it as good for civil rights and a natural evolution of the empire
Should be kept for sentimental and historical reasons once the economy allows
She should publicly embrace and flaunt her Blackness rather than present as white
His marriage matters less because he is far down the line of succession
She should keep doing her show while Harry lives in LA, then transition to England when it ends

entities: King Edward VIII (referenced obliquely as the king who abdicated)

claims.subject: Meghan Markle
claims.claim: Meghan Markle is the fiancée to Prince Harry.

claims.subject: Meghan Markle
claims.claim: Meghan Markle has dark skin and is half black, with a black mother and a white father.

claims.subject: Meghan Markle
claims.claim: Meghan Markle responded to comments about her ethnicity by saying it's sad that people still talk about it in this day and age.

claims.subject: British Royal Family
claims.claim: The British Royal family is totally dysfunctional.

claims.subject: Meghan Markle
claims.claim: Some people didn't like that Meghan Markle was marrying into the Royal family because she was an actress from LA and had been divorced.

claims.subject: British Royal Family
claims.claim: The British Royal family is the treasure of the human race, not because they're superior, but because they're a museum piece with lineage tracing back to Alfred the Great.

claims.subject: British Royal Family
claims.claim: The British monarchy is the most famous because Britain was a world empire.

claims.subject: British Royal Family
claims.claim: Many people question whether to keep the monarchy because they're obsolete and just figureheads.

claims.subject: British Royal Family
claims.claim: People justify keeping the monarchy because they bring tourism dollars, like visits to Windsor Castle.

claims.subject: British Royal Family
claims.claim: British people are surprisingly divided on keeping the Royal family, with some wanting to keep them for sentimental reasons.

claims.subject: British Royal Family
claims.claim: The Royal family should be kept because, once the economy is fixed, we can afford to have them live in Windsor Castle.

claims.subject: British Royal Family
claims.claim: Keeping the Royal family is like preserving history, similar to meeting Buzz Aldrin and acknowledging his moon landing.

claims.subject: British Royal Family
claims.claim: Most people wouldn't want black people in the Royal bloodline because they want to keep the lineage as exemplary Brits.

claims.subject: Prince Harry
claims.claim: Prince Harry is far enough in the line of succession (number five or six) that his marriage to Meghan Markle won't significantly affect the bloodline.

claims.subject: Meghan Markle
claims.claim: If Meghan Markle embraces her black identity and leads Royal functions as a black queen, it would be good for the Royal family.

claims.subject: British Empire
claims.claim: The British Empire includes America, so Meghan Markle being American fits historically.

claims.subject: Royal Marriages
claims.claim: Historically, Royal families married for Royal ties, not necessarily from within their empire.

claims.subject: Royal Marriages
claims.claim: Prince Harry is following the mold of marrying Royal ties, just in a modern way with an American actress.

claims.subject: King George VI
claims.claim: King George VI had to advocate in the 1950s to marry an American woman who wasn't divorced, similar to Prince Harry's situation.

claims.subject: Meghan Markle
claims.claim: Meghan Markle's divorce from a Hollywood producer is a concern for some, as the Royal family is dysfunctional.

claims.subject: Meghan Markle
claims.claim: Marrying a half-black woman like Meghan Markle is like spicing up British culture.

claims.subject: Black People
claims.claim: Black people are as much a part of the British Empire as white people, and excluding them is racism.

claims.subject: Roman Empire
claims.claim: The Roman Empire included diverse groups like Jews and barbarians, similar to how the British Empire includes black people.

claims.subject: Black Americans
claims.claim: Black Americans have been in America for 250 years and are just as American as anyone else.

claims.subject: Meghan Markle
claims.claim: Having a half-black queen like Meghan Markle is good for civil rights and humanity.

claims.subject: Meghan Markle's Family
claims.claim: Meghan Markle's father went bankrupt and moved to Mexico, and is upset she didn't help him financially.

claims.subject: Meghan Markle's Brother
claims.claim: Meghan Markle's brother says she's a great person and apologized for a drunken incident, calling it a misunderstanding.

claims.subject: Meghan Markle's Mother
claims.claim: Meghan Markle's mother is a yoga instructor and social worker.

claims.subject: Meghan Markle
claims.claim: Meghan Markle should flaunt her blackness to solidify her place in the Royal family.

claims.subject: Queen Elizabeth II
claims.claim: The Queen is reportedly upset about Meghan Markle joining the Royal family.

claims.subject: Queen Elizabeth II
claims.claim: The Queen is lovely and welcoming, as evidenced by her interactions with Meghan Markle.

claims.subject: Meghan Markle
claims.claim: Every time Meghan Markle does humanitarian work, she should present as black Meghan Markle, not white Meghan Markle.

claims.subject: Meghan Markle
claims.claim: Meghan Markle marrying an American is great for America and the Royal family.

claims.subject: Meghan Markle
claims.claim: Meghan Markle should continue her acting career in LA while Prince Harry lives there, then move back to England after the show ends.

claims.subject: British Royal Family
claims.claim: The Royal family should produce historical shows about their own lineage, with members acting in them, to stay creative and relevant.
